title: bidens riparia
intro: Bidens riparia, also known as bur-marigold or beggar's ticks, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy seed heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Bidens riparia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and provide relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Bidens riparia include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to skin conditions and menstrual cramps.
